channel.title: Hexagramas on I Chingon
channel.description: Recent content in Hexagramas on I Chingon

item.title: I Ching Hexagram Lookup Matrix
item.description:
How to Use the I Ching Hexagram Finder
#

This matrix allows you to quickly identify the number of any of the 64 I Ching hexagrams by crossing its two component trigrams.

  • Look for the Upper Trigram (Trigram Above) in the horizontal columns at the top.
  • Look for the Lower Trigram (Trigram Below) in the vertical rows on the left.
  • The point of intersection will yield the hexagram number according to King Wen’s traditional arrangement (後天八卦 - Hòutiān Bāguà; Later Heaven, or the Manifested World of Senses).

📌 Navigation Note: The links on each hexagram number will direct you to my Spanish translation of Richard Wilhelm’s traditional text. Within each individual article, you will also find the link to the original German source text for academic reference.

item.title: Advanced I Ching Hexagram Correspondence Table
item.description:
About this Structural Table
#

This matrix crosses King Wen’s classical ordering with its English names, Pinyin, and Simplified Chinese, along with a brief descriptor of its essence, its internal Nuclear Hexagram, and its archetypal structural correspondence in the Earlier Heaven arrangement (先天八卦 - Xiān Tiān Bāguà, or 伏羲八卦 - Fú Xī Bāguà, the binary ordering according to Emperor Fu Xi).

item.title: Hexagram 1 乾 - Qián / THE CREATIVE
item.description:
䷀

Trigrams
#

  • Above: Qián, The Creative, Heaven
  • Below: Qián, The Creative, Heaven

The hexagram consists of six undivided lines. The undivided lines correspond to the light, strong, spiritual, and active primal power. The character of the hexagram is entirely uniform and strong in its nature. Since no weakness adheres to it, its essential quality is power. Its image is heaven. This power is represented as unbound by specific spatial conditions; therefore, it is conceived as movement. Time is considered the foundation of this movement. Thus, the power of time and the power of perseverance in time - duration - are also comprised within this hexagram.

item.title: Hexagram 2 坤 - Kūn / THE RECEPTIVE
item.description:
䷁

Trigrams
#

  • Above: Kūn, The Receptive, Earth
  • Below: Kūn, The Receptive, Earth

This hexagram consists entirely of yielded (divided) lines. The divided line represents the dark, soft, receptive primal power of Yin. The primary attribute of the hexagram is devotion, and its symbol is the Earth. It serves as the perfect counterpart to The Creative - a complement, not an opposite; a completion, not a conflict. It represents Nature in contrast to Spirit, Earth to Heaven, space to time, and the feminine-maternal to the masculine-paternal. When applied to human affairs, this principle of partnership is found not only in the relationship between man and woman, but also between ruler and minister, or father and son. Even within the individual, this duality manifests as the coexistence of the spiritual and the sensory.
